revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:行者塚古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|coordinates={{Coord|34.781944|N|134.888472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11628707|1=Saijō Kofun Cluster}}}} {{Japanese kofun | name = Gyōjazuka Kofun (行者塚古墳) | image = [[File:Gyojazuka Kofun, funkyu-1.jpg|280px]]
Kofun
(Rear circle on the left, front part on the right, center showing western projection) | Other_name = Chōshi Kofun / Ipponmatsu Kofun | Belongs_to = {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|en|Saijō Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11628707|lt=Saijō Kofun Cluster|1=Saijō Kofun Cluster}} | location = {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|qid=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa|1=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}}, Yamate 2-chome | coordinates = 34 | coordinates = 46 | coordinates = 54.95 | coordinates = 134 | coordinates = 53 | coordinates = 18.10 | omitted = JP-28 | type = {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} | area = Mound length 99 m | burial = Rear circle: 3 clay chambers
Northeast projection: 1 clay chamber | burial goods_found = Iron burial goods, bronze burial goods, {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}} | Associated_sites = | built = Late {{ill|4th century|en|4th century|qid=Q8099|lt=4th century|1=4th century}} to early {{ill|5th century|en|5th century|qid=Q8095|lt=5th century|1=5th century}} | Interred_person = Unknown | Tomb_status = | Historic_site = Included in the national {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}} as part of the Saijō Kofun Cluster | Tangible_notes = | notes = | map = Japan Hyōgo | type = Keyhole-shaped tumulus (southwest view) | map_dot_label = Gyōjazuka Kofun | map_dot_label = top }} '''Gyōjazuka Kofun''' (行者塚古墳) is a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|qid=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}} located in Yamate, {{ill|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|en|Kakogawa, Hyōgo|qid=Q750569|lt=Kakogawa|1=Kakogawa, Hyōgo}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|qid=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. It is one of the Kofuns typeing the {{ill|Saijō Kofun Cluster|en|Saijō Kofun Cluster|qid=Q11628707|lt=Saijō Kofun Cluster|1=Saijō Kofun Cluster}}, a Historic site {{ill|Historic site|en|Historic site|qid=Q1081138|lt=Historic site of Japan|1=Historic site}}}. == Overview == The Gyōjazuka Kofun is a large keyhole-shaped tumulus built on a narrow terrace extending northward from the {{ill|Innami Plain|ja|印南野台地|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Innami Plain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in southern Hyōgo Prefecture. The name "Gyōjazuka" originates from a hermit's hall (Gyōjado) that once stood at the summit of the rear circle. It has undergone multiple archaeological surveys, with extensive excavations conducted in {{ill|1995|en|1995|qid=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}–{{ill|1996|en|1996|qid=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}} uncovering burial goods and clarifying details of the "projections" ({{ill|Tsukuridashi|ja|造出|1=Platform-style projection|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsukuridashi|13=User:Immanuelle/Platform-style projection|qq=}}). The mound is oriented southwest, and its length measures 99 meters, making it the largest in Kakogawa and one of the most prominent in the Harima region. The surface is adorned with {{ill|Fukiishi|en|Fukiishi|qid=Q11621213|lt=fukiishi|1=Fukiishi}} mainly of Ryūzan stone, and a row of {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=haniwa|1=Haniwa}} including morning-glory-shaped variants is present. Four projections are situated at the constricted area and rear circle: two on the sides of the constricted area and two at the rear circle. Among these, the western projection of the constricted area has revealed an arrangement of anthropomorphic haniwa and clay replicas of daily objects. One clay chamber was found in the northeastern projection of the rear circle. Surrounding the mound are a moat and embankment, with an additional outer moat located around the rear circle embankment. The mound dates back to the late 4th century to early 5th century during the middle{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. It is notable for the early horse equipment, a rare find in the Kinai region, and burial goods indicating exchanges with the Korean Peninsula and China. The site was Historic site a national historic site in {{ill|1973|en|1973|qid=Q2477|lt=1973|1=1973}} as part of the Saijō Kofun Cluster. Restoration and preservation work have been conducted to allow public access. == Archaeological History == * {{ill|1926|en|1926|qid=Q5259|lt=1926|1=1926}}: Surveyed by the Hyōgo Prefecture Historic sites, Scenic Spots, and Natural Monuments Committee and reported as "Chōshi Kofun of Yawata Village." * {{ill|Pacific War|en|Pacific War|qid=Q184425|lt=Pacific War|1=Pacific War}}: Used as a military training ground, with trenches reportedly constructed. * {{ill|1970|en|1970|qid=Q2474|lt=1970|1=1970}}: Measured by the local research group, Inami Kai. * {{ill|1973|en|1973|qid=Q2477|lt=1973|1=1973}}: Designated as part of the Saijō Kofun Cluster on June 18. * {{ill|1995|en|1995|qid=Q2068|lt=1995|1=1995}}–{{ill|1996|en|1996|qid=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}: Comprehensive excavation as part of a preservation project. Numerous burial goods unearthed. * {{ill|2008|en|2008|qid=Q2004|lt=2008|1=2008}}–{{ill|2009|en|2009|qid=Q1996|lt=2009|1=2009}}: Site restoration for public display. == Mound Specifications == The mound features a three-tier construction with the following area: * Mound length: 99 m * Rear circle: Diameter 68 m * Front part: Width 55 m Projections: * Western constricted area: Length ~14 m, width 9 m * Eastern constricted area: Length 13 m, width 7.3 m * Rear circle northwest: Length 11.5 m, width 9 m, height ~1 m * Rear circle northeast: Length 12.5 m, width 8.5 m, height ~1 m == burial == The main burial facilities consist of three clay chambers located at the top of the rear circle, positioned along the tumulus axis. Two boxes containing burial goods were discovered near the chambers. == burial goods == Items recovered include: * Iron and bronze burial goods * Horse fittings * Weapons and tools * Haniwa and ceremonial replicas == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kakogawa City General Culture Center Museum|ja|加古川市総合文化センター博物館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kakogawa City General Culture Center Museum|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Houses and displays burial goods from the Gyōjazuka Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == External Links == * {{Cultural database|401|1891|Saijō Kofun Cluster}} * [[Miyayama Site]] - {{ill|Gyōjazuka Kofun|ja|行者塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gyōjazuka Kofun|qq=}} ...5 KB (696 words) - 16:41, 16 2025 * [[Saijō Kofun Cluster]] The {{nihongo|'''Gyōjazuka Kofun'''|'''行者塚古墳'''||}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyho ... 6 KB (828 words) -
